name: New Hampshire Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Allied Health Professionals Board
provider.parentOrganization: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)

address.description: Office of Professional Licensure and Certification, 7 Eagle Square, Concord, NH 03301

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New Hampshire SLP license cost?
Is New Hampshire part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does New Hampshire allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. New Hampshire participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Hampshire without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New Hampshire?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
